body.text:
I closed $470,000 in new revenue. (High-Ticket sales)

The month before? $340,000.

The month before that? $415,000.

I was making great money... But I had no idea what was coming next.

Here's what hit me:

For 7 years, I thought inconsistent revenue was just "part of being an Online Coach."

I thought: "Some months are feast, some months are famine. That's just how it works."

So I kept doing what I was doing. Booking calls. Closing some. Losing others. Never knowing why.

One month I'd close 30 clients. The next month? 22 clients. Same effort. Same messaging. Different results.

I'd explain my offer on calls. Sometimes they'd buy. Sometimes they wouldn't. I couldn't figure out the pattern.

My accountant hated me. My family hated the stress. I hated the uncertainty.

But deep down, I knew: This doesn't scale.

Every time revenue dropped, I'd panic and book more calls, trying to "make up" for the slow month.

Every time revenue spiked, I'd think I'd figured it out—then the next month would tank.

I was trapped in a cycle I couldn't control.

Then something clicked:

The reason my revenue was unpredictable wasn't because my offer was bad.

It's because I didn't know what actually converted.

Think about it:

If you're closing 40% of your calls some months and 20% other months, your offer isn't clear.

If you can't predict how many leads you need to hit your revenue target, you don't have a sales process.

If every close feels like a gamble instead of a precise pattern, your sales process isn't repeatable.

The problem wasn't the market. The problem was I had no conversion data I could 100% rely on.

So I did something different.

I stopped trying to "close better" on calls.

Instead, I created ONE clear sales document that answered every question a prospect would have.

Then I tracked exactly what happened.

How many people read it? How many bought? What was my conversion rate?

I ran the numbers.

And I discovered something shocking:

When I send this document to 100 qualified leads, 23 of them buy.

Every single time.

23%.

That's it. That's the pattern.

If you use an evergreen sales asset instead of a sales call, conversion data becomes predictable:

If you want $50K in revenue, you need 10 sales...

And if you know you can close 23% of your leads with certainty, you know exactly how many leads you need to generate.

100 leads × 23% = 23 sales = $115,000 in revenue.

It's math. Not magic.

No more guessing. No more feast or famine. No more panic.

My revenue is now predictable.

Last month: $1,400,000 (exactly what I planned).

This month: $1,380,000 MTD (exactly what I planned).

The stress is gone. The uncertainty is gone. The panic is gone.

I know exactly what's coming.

That's when I realized:

If your sales process is clear enough, your revenue becomes predictable.

The offer explains itself. The conversion rate is consistent. The math is simple.

And once I figured this out, everything changed:

→ No more "hoping" a month will be good
→ No more stress about inconsistent income
→ No more scrambling to book extra calls when revenue dips
→ No more wondering "why did that close but this didn't?"

I have one sales document. Clear conversion data. Predictable revenue.

My calendar went from chaos to clarity.

My income went from unpredictable to planned.

My stress went from constant to gone.

I wasn't trapped anymore.

This is what No-Phone Offers do.

They're high-ticket offers ($2K-$20K+) crafted so clearly, they convert at a consistent rate in under 5 minutes.

No explanation needed. No customization required. No "it depends" closing.

Just one document. One conversion rate. One predictable revenue number.

The No-Phone Offer Method shows you exactly how to do this:

✅ How to create a sales document that converts consistently
✅ How to track your conversion rate with precision
✅ How to calculate exactly how many leads you need for your revenue goal
✅ How to eliminate feast or famine forever
✅ Real examples of offers that convert at 20%+ rates

This is the same method I used to go from unpredictable revenue to planned revenue—while actually working LESS.

And it's the same method 1,090+ students are using to know exactly what's coming next month.

Right now, you can get the complete training for $9 (normally $97).

Click below. Get instant access. Start building your predictable revenue system today.

→ Get The No-Phone Offer Method for $9 below

30-Day Money-Back Guarantee. 1,090+ verified students. 4.7/5 rating.

P.S. That $1,400,000 month? I didn't work harder. I didn't book more calls. I just knew exactly how many leads I needed to hit that number. That's the power of conversion data.

body.text:
Diego had no audience, no email list, and no idea how to sell anything online. 6 months later he's making $14K/month teaching chess. 

Here’s how: 

He didn’t get any sponsors, he didn’t sign any brand deals, he didn’t go viral and he definitely didn’t post every day. 

Here's what he did instead: 

Diego made a $17 guide called "The Opening Trap Guide." 

It shows intermediate players how to win games in the first 10 moves using traps their opponents won't see coming.

The whole product is 20 pages long and it solves one very specific problem. 

He spends about $30/day running ads to it. 

Someone sees the ad, buys the guide, and wins their next chess game that afternoon.

But here's the part that matters.

After they buy, Diego doesn't get on a call with them, doesn't send a calendar link and doesn't try to convince them of anything.

All he does is send them a few automated emails over the next week he wrote in advance. 

Diego talks about how he thinks about chess, shares some stories and somewhere in there, he mentions his $500 coaching program for players who want to go deeper.

There’s no pitch & no pressure, just an open door…

…and about 8% of his $17 buyers walk through it.

Now here's what I want you to notice: 

Diego didn't wait, didn't spend two years "building an audience" before he let himself sell something and didn't tell himself he needed 10K followers first, or 50K, or whatever number feels safe.

He just built a $17 product, put it in front of people who wanted it, and let the system do the rest.

That’s what the ‘Rapid Ascension System' will allow you to do. 

It will walk you through creating a digital product that’s cheap enough that people buy without thinking too hard and valuable enough that customers get hungry more when they're done.

So if you've been waiting until you have "enough" followers to start making real money...

You might be standing at a door that was never locked.

It’s time to allow people to start walking through it and get the revenue & recognition you deserve.

Click ‘Learn more’ to get started 👉👉👉

body.text:
The 3-step system for turning $17 buyers into $5,000+ clients automatically in 3 hours without trying to go viral, posting content or wasting time on sales calls… 

3 hours. That's it.  

That’s the gap between "I have nothing to sell" and "someone just paid me."

Now, I realize that sounds like the kind of claim you've heard a hundred times from marketing gurus who are better at selling themselves than actually teaching anything useful…

…so I’m going to do something different.

I'm going to walk you through exactly what happens in those 3 hours, step by step. 

And by the end, you'll know whether this is real or not — because you'll be able to picture yourself doing it.

Fair?

First — the AI prompt.

You open it. 

It asks about your expertise, the problems you've solved and the things people always come to you for. 

You answer honestly, in 15 minutes (maybe less). 

Then something kind of wild happens.

The prompt takes everything you said and assembles a complete product concept. 

Title. Price point. Full content outline. Done.

One of our students — former HR manager — had been helping friends negotiate salary raises for years, casually, ovver coffee. 

Never once thought of it as something she could sell.

The AI pulled that out of her head and turned it into "The Salary Negotiation Script Pack: 7 word-for-word scripts for asking for a raise without making it awkward."

She didn't brainstorm that. 

Didn't stare at a blank page wondering what she had to offer. She just answered questions about what she already knew...

...and the prompt connected dots she couldn't see herself.

Next: the landing page.

Template's pre-built. Headline, subheadline, buy button — all there. 

All you have to do is fill in the blanks, connect your payment processor (4-minute walkthrough for that), and the page goes live.

About 60 minutes of actual work. 

Probably less if you're not second-guessing every word.

Then you flip on $20/day in Facebook ads. Launch.

First buyer? Usually a few hours.

That’s how you go from nothing to a live product, getting real customers in roughly 3 hours  or less. 

Now, most people think the story ends here, but it doesn't, and I’m going to tell you why: 

Those $17 buyers? 

Because your digital product got them a quick, tangible win, some of them will want more and they’ll start upgrading.

To your $2,500 offers, your $5,000 offers or your $10,000 offers, automatically, through a built-in ascension sequence that runs (whether you're awake or not).

You're not on calls and you’re not in anyone's DMs trying to convince them.

Your digital product that took you about 3 hours total to build already did the convincing — they experienced your expertise firsthand. 

Now they want more.

That's the part most "monetize your knowledge" programs completely miss.

They teach you how to create a product. 

Maybe even how to sell it once.

But they leave you stuck at $17 sales forever... because they don't understand the psychology of why people upgrade.

We do.

Diego Villanueva teaches chess. He built his first Digital Snack, ran the ads, and now he does $14K/month.

You're still reading this.

Which means one of two things. 

Either you're genuinely curious whether this could work for you... or you're looking for the catch.

Either way, you wouldn't still be here if some part of you didn't already know you have knowledge worth packaging.

The question isn't whether you have something valuable.

The question is whether you're going to keep letting it sit in your head — or finally turn it into something that pays you while you sleep.

You’re a smart person, so you already know the right answer.

Click the ‘Learn more’ button to get started 👉👉👉

body.text:
If you're an expert already making $10K, $20K, maybe $30K/month...what got you here won't get you to $100K/month.

And you probably already know that deep down.

You've felt it for a while now, haven't you?

That nagging feeling that something's off...

That you're working harder than you should be for the money you're making…

That there's a ceiling above you and no matter how much you push, it won't budge.

Here's what's going on (and what you can do about it):

The hustle that built your business is the same thing KEEPING you stuck.

I know that sounds backwards, but stay with me for a second.

You see, you got here by being good at what you do. 

By showing up when others didn't, by personally selling and by getting on calls and closing deals yourself.

That worked. 

You should be proud of it, but…

A different problem walked in the door:

You wake up and your calendar is already full. 

Calls stacked on calls stacked on calls. 

The revenue doesn't show up unless you show up. 

And while one month you made good money…

The next month you have to make it all over again, from scratch, from the same audience that's getting a liiiittle more tired of being sold to.

That's a job you created that happens to own you. 

And here's what makes this particularly frustrating...

You've watched other experts - some of them honestly less talented than you - figure something out that lets them scale past you. 

They're not working more hours, but somehow they broke through the ceiling you're still hitting.

So what did they figure out?

They stopped being the engine and they built a second one.

See, most experts try to scale by doing MORE of what's already working. More content. More launches. More calls. More hours. They're playing harder within the same game.

But the experts who actually break $100K/month? 

They changed the rules.

Instead of only selling to their existing audience, they built an automated acquisition system that brings in new buyers every single day—without launches, without calls, without personal energy.

It starts with a $17 product that solves one specific problem. 

Something small enough that complete strangers buy it without needing to know who you are. 

Then they run cheap ads to it, allowing them to acquire 50, 100, 200 new buyers every single day. 

That’s how they build a list of buyers who already paid them, trust them.

Those buyers get a short automated email sequence over the next week. 

Nothing aggressive. 

Just more value, more insight, and eventually an invitation to go deeper with a higher-ticket offer.

The $17 product acquires customers while you sleep…

The emails upgrade them while you sleep…

And you wake up to Stripe notifications you didn't personally create.

That's the second engine.

One of our members - a business consultant - was stuck at $27K/month for almost a year. 

He couldn't figure out how to grow without taking more calls. 

60 days after installing this system, he crossed $80K.

Not because he worked harder, because he finally had something working when he wasn't.

That's what I'm going to show you inside Digital Snacks.

I’m going to give you everything you need to install the growth engine under your business that allows our students to scale (see the next page). 

Click the ‘Learn more’ button to get it 👉👉👉

body.text:
Every expert is optimizing for getting sales calls booked. I deleted mine completely and I made more than ever. Here's why:

I added up the hours once.

14 calls a week, about 45 minutes each when you factored in the small talk, the "tell me about your situation" part, the pitch, and the follow-up message afterward where I'd promise to "send over the details."

That's over 10 hours a week. 

That’s more than a full workday, every week, spent trying to convince semi-interested people to buy.

My close rate sat around 25%.

 So out of those 14 calls, maybe 3 or 4 would actually say yes. 

The rest needed to "think about it" or "talk to their spouse" or "check back next month."

Well, they never checked back.

About a year ago I stopped doing calls entirely, and did something better that allowed me to make well over 6-figures last year while finally having the freedom I want.

What I do instead: sell a $17 product up front. 

A Digital Snack — solves one specific problem, takes someone about 20 minutes to go through.

When a person pays $17 and gets an actual result from it, they want more.

Just like the first bite of Doritos makes you hungry for more, the first win gets them hungry for more.

As a result, you stop being "some person trying to sell me coaching" and become "the person whose stuff actually works." 

That’s why, when my high-ticket offer shows up in the automated upgrade sequence a few days later, the internal conversation is completely different and that’s why they upgrade themselves, automatically. 

I don't pitch. I don't hop on a call. 

In fact, half the time I don't even know it happened until I open Stripe.

I tested how far this could go. 

So I went to the Sahara Desert for 5 days — zero cell service, zero WiFi, zero access to anything…

…and came back to 612 new sales and $25K sitting in my account. 

The system didn't need me to be present, let alone on a phone call.

My revenue actually went up when I quit doing calls. 

That still catches me off guard when I say it out loud.

I traded 10 hours a week of convincing for a system that runs the same process automatically, around the clock.

Digital Snacks shows you exactly how to set this up, step-by-step. 

The $17 product, the landing page, the ads, the upgrade sequence — everything.

So if you're spending your weeks on Zoom with people who mostly aren't going to buy anyway... 

Let me hand you my system that will get you out of it. 

Click the ‘Learn more’ button to get started 👉👉👉

cards.body:
Diego had no audience, no email list, and no idea how to sell anything online. 6 months later he's making $14K/month teaching chess. 

Here’s how: 

He didn’t get any sponsors, he didn’t sign any brand deals, he didn’t go viral and he definitely didn’t post every day. 

Here's what he did instead: 

Diego made a $17 guide called "The Opening Trap Guide." 

It shows intermediate players how to win games in the first 10 moves using traps their opponents won't see coming.

The whole product is 20 pages long and it solves one very specific problem. 

He spends about $30/day running ads to it. 

Someone sees the ad, buys the guide, and wins their next chess game that afternoon.

But here's the part that matters.

After they buy, Diego doesn't get on a call with them, doesn't send a calendar link and doesn't try to convince them of anything.

All he does is send them a few automated emails over the next week he wrote in advance. 

Diego talks about how he thinks about chess, shares some stories and somewhere in there, he mentions his $500 coaching program for players who want to go deeper.

There’s no pitch & no pressure, just an open door…

…and about 8% of his $17 buyers walk through it.

Now here's what I want you to notice: 

Diego didn't wait, didn't spend two years "building an audience" before he let himself sell something and didn't tell himself he needed 10K followers first, or 50K, or whatever number feels safe.

He just built a $17 product, put it in front of people who wanted it, and let the system do the rest.

That’s what the ‘Rapid Ascension System' will allow you to do. 

It will walk you through creating a digital product that’s cheap enough that people buy without thinking too hard and valuable enough that customers get hungry more when they're done.

So if you've been waiting until you have "enough" followers to start making real money...

You might be standing at a door that was never locked.

It’s time to allow people to start walking through it and get the revenue & recognition you deserve.

Click ‘Learn more’ to get started 👉👉👉

body.text:
I don't chase high-ticket clients, haven't in over a year now and I still made $750,000 last year. What I do instead sounds a little backwards, so bear with me.

I sell a $17 product.

One, simple digital product meant to solve one very specific problem. 

It takes people about 20 minutes to go through and get a result from it.

Within about 7 days, a chunk of those $17 buyers upgrade themselves to $5,000-$10,000.

No sales calls. No "hop on a quick Zoom so I can feel you out." No convincing whatsoever.

Here's why this works (and why going straight from free content to high-ticket almost never does) 

When someone follows you for free, you're entertainment. 

You're a scroll-past. 

The second you ask them for $5,000, there's this massive trust gap you have to close. 

That's why you end up on 45-minute sales calls doing dog-and-pony shows for people who were never going to buy anyway.

But when someone pays you $17 and actually gets a quick win from it?

Something shifts.

They stop thinking of you as "some person posting content" and start thinking "this person's paid stuff actually works." 

Now there's a transaction history. 

There's trust with a receipt attached to it.

So when they see a $2,500 offer from you the following week, the internal conversation isn't "can I trust this person?" 

It's "the $17 thing worked, so..."

That's the whole game. 

The first payment does the heavy lifting. 

Everything after that has almost no friction.

I call these $17 products "Digital Snacks." 

You take one problem you've already solved in your area of expertise — something that's just sitting in your head right now — and you spend about an hour packaging it using AI prompts and plug-and-play templates.

Then you build a stupid simple landing page for it. Headline, subheadline, buy button. That's the whole thing. 

Then you run $20/day in Facebook ads. 

And within hours, people start buying. 

Then the system's built-in upgrade sequence handles the rest. Automatic. No DMs. No calls.

I tested this by going to the Sahara Desert for 5 days with zero cell service. 

Came back to 612 sales and $25K sitting in Stripe. 

Nothing needed my attention. 

Nothing broke.

Best of all, it works in the least glamorous markets as well. 

One of our students, Diego Villanueva, teaches people how to get better at chess and he does $14K/month with this. 

Starr Codd pulled in $18,600 her first week. She didn't even run ads.

1,090+ people are using the Digital Snacks system right now.

The full thing is $17 during our launch period. Normally $147 — we dropped the price temporarily while we built out more case studies. 

That price goes back up once we have enough.

If you're a coach, creator, or expert who's been giving away your best knowledge for free hoping it eventually turns into revenue... this is the part where that stops.

Click the ‘Learn more’ button to get it 👉👉👉

body.text:
The 3-step system for turning $17 buyers into $5,000+ clients automatically in 3 hours without trying to go viral, posting content or wasting time on sales calls… 

3 hours. That's it.  

That’s the gap between "I have nothing to sell" and "someone just paid me."

Now, I realize that sounds like the kind of claim you've heard a hundred times from marketing gurus who are better at selling themselves than actually teaching anything useful…

…so I’m going to do something different.

I'm going to walk you through exactly what happens in those 3 hours, step by step. 

And by the end, you'll know whether this is real or not — because you'll be able to picture yourself doing it.

Fair?

First — the AI prompt.

You open it. 

It asks about your expertise, the problems you've solved and the things people always come to you for. 

You answer honestly, in 15 minutes (maybe less). 

Then something kind of wild happens.

The prompt takes everything you said and assembles a complete product concept. 

Title. Price point. Full content outline. Done.

One of our students — former HR manager — had been helping friends negotiate salary raises for years, casually, ovver coffee. 

Never once thought of it as something she could sell.

The AI pulled that out of her head and turned it into "The Salary Negotiation Script Pack: 7 word-for-word scripts for asking for a raise without making it awkward."

She didn't brainstorm that. 

Didn't stare at a blank page wondering what she had to offer. She just answered questions about what she already knew...

...and the prompt connected dots she couldn't see herself.

Next: the landing page.

Template's pre-built. Headline, subheadline, buy button — all there. 

All you have to do is fill in the blanks, connect your payment processor (4-minute walkthrough for that), and the page goes live.

About 60 minutes of actual work. 

Probably less if you're not second-guessing every word.

Then you flip on $20/day in Facebook ads. Launch.

First buyer? Usually a few hours.

That’s how you go from nothing to a live product, getting real customers in roughly 3 hours  or less. 

Now, most people think the story ends here, but it doesn't, and I’m going to tell you why: 

Those $17 buyers? 

Because your digital product got them a quick, tangible win, some of them will want more and they’ll start upgrading.

To your $2,500 offers, your $5,000 offers or your $10,000 offers, automatically, through a built-in ascension sequence that runs (whether you're awake or not).

You're not on calls and you’re not in anyone's DMs trying to convince them.

Your digital product that took you about 3 hours total to build already did the convincing — they experienced your expertise firsthand. 

Now they want more.

That's the part most "monetize your knowledge" programs completely miss.

They teach you how to create a product. 

Maybe even how to sell it once.

But they leave you stuck at $17 sales forever... because they don't understand the psychology of why people upgrade.

We do.

Diego Villanueva teaches chess. He built his first Digital Snack, ran the ads, and now he does $14K/month.

You're still reading this.

Which means one of two things. 

Either you're genuinely curious whether this could work for you... or you're looking for the catch.

Either way, you wouldn't still be here if some part of you didn't already know you have knowledge worth packaging.

The question isn't whether you have something valuable.

The question is whether you're going to keep letting it sit in your head — or finally turn it into something that pays you while you sleep.

You’re a smart person, so you already know the right answer.

Click the ‘Learn more’ button to get started 👉👉👉

body.text:
I don't chase high-ticket clients, haven't in over a year now and I still made $750,000 last year. What I do instead sounds a little backwards, so bear with me.

I sell a $17 product.

One, simple digital product meant to solve one very specific problem. 

It takes people about 20 minutes to go through and get a result from it.

Within about 7 days, a chunk of those $17 buyers upgrade themselves to $5,000-$10,000.

No sales calls. No "hop on a quick Zoom so I can feel you out." No convincing whatsoever.

Here's why this works (and why going straight from free content to high-ticket almost never does) 

When someone follows you for free, you're entertainment. 

You're a scroll-past. 

The second you ask them for $5,000, there's this massive trust gap you have to close. 

That's why you end up on 45-minute sales calls doing dog-and-pony shows for people who were never going to buy anyway.

But when someone pays you $17 and actually gets a quick win from it?

Something shifts.

They stop thinking of you as "some person posting content" and start thinking "this person's paid stuff actually works." 

Now there's a transaction history. 

There's trust with a receipt attached to it.

So when they see a $2,500 offer from you the following week, the internal conversation isn't "can I trust this person?" 

It's "the $17 thing worked, so..."

That's the whole game. 

The first payment does the heavy lifting. 

Everything after that has almost no friction.

I call these $17 products "Digital Snacks." 

You take one problem you've already solved in your area of expertise — something that's just sitting in your head right now — and you spend about an hour packaging it using AI prompts and plug-and-play templates.

Then you build a stupid simple landing page for it. Headline, subheadline, buy button. That's the whole thing. 

Then you run $20/day in Facebook ads. 

And within hours, people start buying. 

Then the system's built-in upgrade sequence handles the rest. Automatic. No DMs. No calls.

I tested this by going to the Sahara Desert for 5 days with zero cell service. 

Came back to 612 sales and $25K sitting in Stripe. 

Nothing needed my attention. 

Nothing broke.

One of our students, Diego Villanueva, teaches people how to get better at chess and he does $14K/month with this. 

Starr Codd pulled in $18,600 her first week. She didn't even run ads.

body.text:
Every expert is optimizing for getting sales calls booked. I deleted mine completely and I made more than ever. Here's why:

I added up the hours once.

14 calls a week, about 45 minutes each when you factored in the small talk, the "tell me about your situation" part, the pitch, and the follow-up message afterward where I'd promise to "send over the details."

That's over 10 hours a week. 

That’s more than a full workday, every week, spent trying to convince semi-interested people to buy.

My close rate sat around 25%.

 So out of those 14 calls, maybe 3 or 4 would actually say yes. 

The rest needed to "think about it" or "talk to their spouse" or "check back next month."

Well, they never checked back.

About a year ago I stopped doing calls entirely, and did something better that allowed me to make well over 6-figures last year while finally having the freedom I want.

What I do instead: sell a $17 product up front. 

A Digital Snack — solves one specific problem, takes someone about 20 minutes to go through.

When a person pays $17 and gets an actual result from it, they want more.

Just like the first bite of Doritos makes you hungry for more, the first win gets them hungry for more.

As a result, you stop being "some person trying to sell me coaching" and become "the person whose stuff actually works." 

That’s why, when my high-ticket offer shows up in the automated upgrade sequence a few days later, the internal conversation is completely different and that’s why they upgrade themselves, automatically. 

I don't pitch. I don't hop on a call. 

In fact, half the time I don't even know it happened until I open Stripe.

I tested how far this could go. 

So I went to the Sahara Desert for 5 days — zero cell service, zero WiFi, zero access to anything…

…and came back to 612 new sales and $25K sitting in my account. 

The system didn't need me to be present, let alone on a phone call.

My revenue actually went up when I quit doing calls. 

That still catches me off guard when I say it out loud.

I traded 10 hours a week of convincing for a system that runs the same process automatically, around the clock.

Digital Snacks shows you exactly how to set this up, step-by-step. 

The $17 product, the landing page, the ads, the upgrade sequence — everything.

So if you're spending your weeks on Zoom with people who mostly aren't going to buy anyway... 

Let me hand you my system that will get you out of it. 

Click the ‘Learn more’ button to get started 👉👉👉

body.text:
The 3-step system for turning $17 buyers into $5,000+ clients automatically in 3 hours without trying to go viral, posting content or wasting time on sales calls… 

3 hours. That's it.  

That’s the gap between "I have nothing to sell" and "someone just paid me."

Now, I realize that sounds like the kind of claim you've heard a hundred times from marketing gurus who are better at selling themselves than actually teaching anything useful…

…so I’m going to do something different.

I'm going to walk you through exactly what happens in those 3 hours, step by step. 

And by the end, you'll know whether this is real or not — because you'll be able to picture yourself doing it.

Fair?

First — the AI prompt.

You open it. 

It asks about your expertise, the problems you've solved and the things people always come to you for. 

You answer honestly, in 15 minutes (maybe less). 

Then something kind of wild happens.

The prompt takes everything you said and assembles a complete product concept. 

Title. Price point. Full content outline. Done.

One of our students — former HR manager — had been helping friends negotiate salary raises for years, casually, ovver coffee. 

Never once thought of it as something she could sell.

The AI pulled that out of her head and turned it into "The Salary Negotiation Script Pack: 7 word-for-word scripts for asking for a raise without making it awkward."

She didn't brainstorm that. 

Didn't stare at a blank page wondering what she had to offer. She just answered questions about what she already knew...

...and the prompt connected dots she couldn't see herself.

Next: the landing page.

Template's pre-built. Headline, subheadline, buy button — all there. 

All you have to do is fill in the blanks, connect your payment processor (4-minute walkthrough for that), and the page goes live.

About 60 minutes of actual work. 

Probably less if you're not second-guessing every word.

Then you flip on $20/day in Facebook ads. Launch.

First buyer? Usually a few hours.

That’s how you go from nothing to a live product, getting real customers in roughly 3 hours  or less. 

Now, most people think the story ends here, but it doesn't, and I’m going to tell you why: 

Those $17 buyers? 

Because your digital product got them a quick, tangible win, some of them will want more and they’ll start upgrading.

To your $2,500 offers, your $5,000 offers or your $10,000 offers, automatically, through a built-in ascension sequence that runs (whether you're awake or not).

You're not on calls and you’re not in anyone's DMs trying to convince them.

Your digital product that took you about 3 hours total to build already did the convincing — they experienced your expertise firsthand. 

Now they want more.

That's the part most "monetize your knowledge" programs completely miss.

They teach you how to create a product. 

Maybe even how to sell it once.

But they leave you stuck at $17 sales forever... because they don't understand the psychology of why people upgrade.

We do.

Diego Villanueva teaches chess. He built his first Digital Snack, ran the ads, and now he does $14K/month.

You're still reading this.

Which means one of two things. 

Either you're genuinely curious whether this could work for you... or you're looking for the catch.

Either way, you wouldn't still be here if some part of you didn't already know you have knowledge worth packaging.

The question isn't whether you have something valuable.

The question is whether you're going to keep letting it sit in your head — or finally turn it into something that pays you while you sleep.

You’re a smart person, so you already know the right answer.

Click the ‘Learn more’ button to get started 👉👉👉

body.text:
Diego had no audience, no email list, and no idea how to sell anything online. 6 months later he's making $14K/month teaching chess. 

Here’s how: 

He didn’t get any sponsors, he didn’t sign any brand deals, he didn’t go viral and he definitely didn’t post every day. 

Here's what he did instead: 

Diego made a $17 guide called "The Opening Trap Guide." 

It shows intermediate players how to win games in the first 10 moves using traps their opponents won't see coming.

The whole product is 20 pages long and it solves one very specific problem. 

He spends about $30/day running ads to it. 

Someone sees the ad, buys the guide, and wins their next chess game that afternoon.

But here's the part that matters.

After they buy, Diego doesn't get on a call with them, doesn't send a calendar link and doesn't try to convince them of anything.

All he does is send them a few automated emails over the next week he wrote in advance. 

Diego talks about how he thinks about chess, shares some stories and somewhere in there, he mentions his $500 coaching program for players who want to go deeper.

There’s no pitch & no pressure, just an open door…

…and about 8% of his $17 buyers walk through it.

Now here's what I want you to notice: 

Diego didn't wait, didn't spend two years "building an audience" before he let himself sell something and didn't tell himself he needed 10K followers first, or 50K, or whatever number feels safe.

He just built a $17 product, put it in front of people who wanted it, and let the system do the rest.

That’s what the ‘Rapid Ascension System' will allow you to do. 

It will walk you through creating a digital product that’s cheap enough that people buy without thinking too hard and valuable enough that customers get hungry more when they're done.

So if you've been waiting until you have "enough" followers to start making real money...

You might be standing at a door that was never locked.

It’s time to allow people to start walking through it and get the revenue & recognition you deserve.

Click ‘Learn more’ to get started 👉👉👉

snapshot.title: How $17 Buyers Become High Ticket Clients In 7 Days (No Sales Calls)

snapshot.caption: phonefreesales.co

body.text:
I removed sales calls from my business 6 months ago – my close rate more than doubled.

I'm a great coach. 

I know that because my clients get results. 

They refer friends, they stay for months, they re-enroll.

But put me on a sales call… and something breaks.

My voice gets tight, I talk too fast, I start over-explaining things nobody asked about and I can feel the prospect pulling away, so I over-explain MORE — which only makes it worse.

For 3 years I thought the answer was getting better at sales calls. 

So I bought a closing course. 

Then another one. 

Practiced objection handling in front of my bathroom mirror like some kind of lunatic.

And while I got slightly better… I disliked every second of it.

My prospects could smell it, too. 

My close rate sat around 15%, which is pretty rough when your whole income depends on it.

One night after a particularly bad call — the kind where you hang up and just sit there replaying everything you said wrong — I started thinking about why my coaching sessions felt so natural but sales calls felt like torture.

And I realized something obvious that I'd been ignoring for years.

In my coaching sessions, I prepare.

I think through what I'm going to say, I write notes and I organize my thoughts. 

Everything comes out clear and confident because I've already worked through it in writing. 

On sales calls? 

I wing it and hope my personality carries me. It doesn't.

So I asked myself a question that probably should've been obvious a lot sooner: 

What if I just stopped selling on the phone and sold the way I actually communicate well?

And that’s exactly what I did.

I created one clear sales document that laid out four things: 

👉 The exact problem I solve
👉 The exact mechanism I use
👉 The exact outcome clients get
👉 And the exact process we'd follow together.

That's it. 

No pitch and no performance. 

Just the information someone actually needs to make a decision.

If they have questions, they send me a message and I answer them. 

If it's right, they enroll. 

If not, neither of us wasted an hour pretending to enjoy a Zoom call.

Within 3 months, my close rate went from 15% to 38%.

The only thing that changed was the format.

And something else changed that I didn't expect — my authority went UP. 

Turns out, when you stop chasing people on calls and let a document speak for you…

Prospects treat you differently – and you feel like an expert again (instead of a salesperson auditioning for a job).

A handful of other coaches saw what I was doing and asked me to walk them through it. 

Enough of them asked that I eventually put the whole process into a course.

It's called the No Phone Offer.

It'll walk you through building a sales document that does the closing for you…

One that makes your offer so clear that prospects stop asking "but will it work for me?" and start asking "how do I enroll?"

All you have to do is follow the process and you’ll get a clear, written offer that enrolls high-ticket clients for you 24/7 – even when you're not on the phone.

If you're someone who's genuinely good at what you do but gets a knot in your stomach before every discovery call or just doesn't want to waste your time on sales calls…

Click 'Learn more' to find out more about it 👉👉👉

body.text:
I don't chase high-ticket clients, haven't in over a year now and I still made $750,000 last year. What I do instead sounds a little backwards, so bear with me.

I sell a $17 product.

One, simple digital product meant to solve one very specific problem. 

It takes people about 20 minutes to go through and get a result from it.

Within about 7 days, a chunk of those $17 buyers upgrade themselves to $5,000-$10,000.

No sales calls. No "hop on a quick Zoom so I can feel you out." No convincing whatsoever.

Here's why this works (and why going straight from free content to high-ticket almost never does) 

When someone follows you for free, you're entertainment. 

You're a scroll-past. 

The second you ask them for $5,000, there's this massive trust gap you have to close. 

That's why you end up on 45-minute sales calls doing dog-and-pony shows for people who were never going to buy anyway.

But when someone pays you $17 and actually gets a quick win from it?

Something shifts.

They stop thinking of you as "some person posting content" and start thinking "this person's paid stuff actually works." 

Now there's a transaction history. 

There's trust with a receipt attached to it.

So when they see a $2,500 offer from you the following week, the internal conversation isn't "can I trust this person?" 

It's "the $17 thing worked, so..."

That's the whole game. 

The first payment does the heavy lifting. 

Everything after that has almost no friction.

I call these $17 products "Digital Snacks." 

You take one problem you've already solved in your area of expertise — something that's just sitting in your head right now — and you spend about an hour packaging it using AI prompts and plug-and-play templates.

Then you build a stupid simple landing page for it. Headline, subheadline, buy button. That's the whole thing. 

Then you run $20/day in Facebook ads. 

And within hours, people start buying. 

Then the system's built-in upgrade sequence handles the rest. Automatic. No DMs. No calls.

I tested this by going to the Sahara Desert for 5 days with zero cell service. 

Came back to 612 sales and $25K sitting in Stripe. 

Nothing needed my attention. 

Nothing broke.

Best of all, it works in the least glamorous markets as well. 

One of our students, Diego Villanueva, teaches people how to get better at chess and he does $14K/month with this. 

Starr Codd pulled in $18,600 her first week. She didn't even run ads.

1,090+ people are using the Digital Snacks system right now.

The full thing is $17 during our launch period. Normally $147 — we dropped the price temporarily while we built out more case studies. 

That price goes back up once we have enough.

If you're a coach, creator, or expert who's been giving away your best knowledge for free hoping it eventually turns into revenue... this is the part where that stops.

Click the ‘Learn more’ button to get it 👉👉👉

body.text:
Every expert is optimizing for getting sales calls booked. I deleted mine completely and I made more than ever. Here's why:

I added up the hours once.

14 calls a week, about 45 minutes each when you factored in the small talk, the "tell me about your situation" part, the pitch, and the follow-up message afterward where I'd promise to "send over the details."

That's over 10 hours a week. 

That’s more than a full workday, every week, spent trying to convince semi-interested people to buy.

My close rate sat around 25%.

 So out of those 14 calls, maybe 3 or 4 would actually say yes. 

The rest needed to "think about it" or "talk to their spouse" or "check back next month."

Well, they never checked back.

About a year ago I stopped doing calls entirely, and did something better that allowed me to make well over 6-figures last year while finally having the freedom I want.

What I do instead: sell a $17 product up front. 

A Digital Snack — solves one specific problem, takes someone about 20 minutes to go through.

When a person pays $17 and gets an actual result from it, they want more.

Just like the first bite of Doritos makes you hungry for more, the first win gets them hungry for more.

As a result, you stop being "some person trying to sell me coaching" and become "the person whose stuff actually works." 

That’s why, when my high-ticket offer shows up in the automated upgrade sequence a few days later, the internal conversation is completely different and that’s why they upgrade themselves, automatically. 

I don't pitch. I don't hop on a call. 

In fact, half the time I don't even know it happened until I open Stripe.

I tested how far this could go. 

So I went to the Sahara Desert for 5 days — zero cell service, zero WiFi, zero access to anything…

…and came back to 612 new sales and $25K sitting in my account. 

The system didn't need me to be present, let alone on a phone call.

My revenue actually went up when I quit doing calls. 

That still catches me off guard when I say it out loud.

I traded 10 hours a week of convincing for a system that runs the same process automatically, around the clock.

Digital Snacks shows you exactly how to set this up, step-by-step. 

The $17 product, the landing page, the ads, the upgrade sequence — everything.

So if you're spending your weeks on Zoom with people who mostly aren't going to buy anyway... 

Let me hand you my system that will get you out of it. 

Click the ‘Learn more’ button to get started 👉👉👉

body.text:
If you're an expert already making $10K, $20K, maybe $30K/month...what got you here won't get you to $100K/month.

And you probably already know that deep down.

You've felt it for a while now, haven't you?

That nagging feeling that something's off...

That you're working harder than you should be for the money you're making…

That there's a ceiling above you and no matter how much you push, it won't budge.

Here's what's going on (and what you can do about it):

The hustle that built your business is the same thing KEEPING you stuck.

I know that sounds backwards, but stay with me for a second.

You see, you got here by being good at what you do. 

By showing up when others didn't, by personally selling and by getting on calls and closing deals yourself.

That worked. 

You should be proud of it, but…

A different problem walked in the door:

You wake up and your calendar is already full. 

Calls stacked on calls stacked on calls. 

The revenue doesn't show up unless you show up. 

And while one month you made good money…

The next month you have to make it all over again, from scratch, from the same audience that's getting a liiiittle more tired of being sold to.

That's a job you created that happens to own you. 

And here's what makes this particularly frustrating...

You've watched other experts - some of them honestly less talented than you - figure something out that lets them scale past you. 

They're not working more hours, but somehow they broke through the ceiling you're still hitting.

So what did they figure out?

They stopped being the engine and they built a second one.

See, most experts try to scale by doing MORE of what's already working. More content. More launches. More calls. More hours. They're playing harder within the same game.

But the experts who actually break $100K/month? 

They changed the rules.

Instead of only selling to their existing audience, they built an automated acquisition system that brings in new buyers every single day—without launches, without calls, without personal energy.

It starts with a $17 product that solves one specific problem. 

Something small enough that complete strangers buy it without needing to know who you are. 

Then they run cheap ads to it, allowing them to acquire 50, 100, 200 new buyers every single day. 

That’s how they build a list of buyers who already paid them, trust them.

Those buyers get a short automated email sequence over the next week. 

Nothing aggressive. 

Just more value, more insight, and eventually an invitation to go deeper with a higher-ticket offer.

The $17 product acquires customers while you sleep…

The emails upgrade them while you sleep…

And you wake up to Stripe notifications you didn't personally create.

That's the second engine.

One of our members - a business consultant - was stuck at $27K/month for almost a year. 

He couldn't figure out how to grow without taking more calls. 

60 days after installing this system, he crossed $80K.

Not because he worked harder, because he finally had something working when he wasn't.

That's what I'm going to show you inside Digital Snacks.

I’m going to give you everything you need to install the growth engine under your business that allows our students to scale (see the next page). 

Click the ‘Learn more’ button to get it 👉👉👉

snapshot.linkDescription: AI-powered system. 1,090+ students. Build your first Digital Snack in 60 minutes. $17 today.
snapshot.title: How $17 Buyers Become High Ticket Clients In 7 Days (No Sales Calls)

body.text:
Diego had no audience, no email list, and no idea how to sell anything online. 6 months later he's making $14K/month teaching chess. 

Here’s how: 

He didn’t get any sponsors, he didn’t sign any brand deals, he didn’t go viral and he definitely didn’t post every day. 

Here's what he did instead: 

Diego made a $17 guide called "The Opening Trap Guide." 

It shows intermediate players how to win games in the first 10 moves using traps their opponents won't see coming.

The whole product is 20 pages long and it solves one very specific problem. 

He spends about $30/day running ads to it. 

Someone sees the ad, buys the guide, and wins their next chess game that afternoon.

But here's the part that matters.

After they buy, Diego doesn't get on a call with them, doesn't send a calendar link and doesn't try to convince them of anything.

All he does is send them a few automated emails over the next week he wrote in advance. 

Diego talks about how he thinks about chess, shares some stories and somewhere in there, he mentions his $500 coaching program for players who want to go deeper.

There’s no pitch & no pressure, just an open door…

…and about 8% of his $17 buyers walk through it.

Now here's what I want you to notice: 

Diego didn't wait, didn't spend two years "building an audience" before he let himself sell something and didn't tell himself he needed 10K followers first, or 50K, or whatever number feels safe.

He just built a $17 product, put it in front of people who wanted it, and let the system do the rest.

That’s what the ‘Rapid Ascension System' will allow you to do. 

It will walk you through creating a digital product that’s cheap enough that people buy without thinking too hard and valuable enough that customers get hungry more when they're done.

So if you've been waiting until you have "enough" followers to start making real money...

You might be standing at a door that was never locked.

It’s time to allow people to start walking through it and get the revenue & recognition you deserve.

Click ‘Learn more’ to get started 👉👉👉

They refer friends, they stay for months, they re-enroll.

But put me on a sales call… and something breaks.

My voice gets tight, I talk too fast, I start over-explaining things nobody asked about and I can feel the prospect pulling away, so I over-explain MORE — which only makes it worse.

For 3 years I thought the answer was getting better at sales calls. 

So I bought a closing course. 

Then another one. 

Practiced objection handling in front of my bathroom mirror like some kind of lunatic.

And while I got slightly better… I disliked every second of it.

My prospects could smell it, too. 

My close rate sat around 15%, which is pretty rough when your whole income depends on it.

One night after a particularly bad call — the kind where you hang up and just sit there replaying everything you said wrong — I started thinking about why my coaching sessions felt so natural but sales calls felt like torture.

And I realized something obvious that I'd been ignoring for years.

In my coaching sessions, I prepare.

I think through what I'm going to say, I write notes and I organize my thoughts. 

Everything comes out clear and confident because I've already worked through it in writing. 

On sales calls? 

I wing it and hope my personality carries me. It doesn't.

So I asked myself a question that probably should've been obvious a lot sooner: 

What if I just stopped selling on the phone and sold the way I actually communicate well?

And that’s exactly what I did.

I created one clear sales document that laid out four things: 

👉 The exact problem I solve
👉 The exact mechanism I use
👉 The exact outcome clients get
👉 And the exact process we'd follow together.

That's it. 

No pitch and no performance. 

Just the information someone actually needs to make a decision.

If they have questions, they send me a message and I answer them. 

If it's right, they enroll. 

If not, neither of us wasted an hour pretending to enjoy a Zoom call.

Within 3 months, my close rate went from 15% to 38%.

The only thing that changed was the format.

And something else changed that I didn't expect — my authority went UP. 

Turns out, when you stop chasing people on calls and let a document speak for you…

Prospects treat you differently – and you feel like an expert again (instead of a salesperson auditioning for a job).

A handful of other coaches saw what I was doing and asked me to walk them through it. 

Enough of them asked that I eventually put the whole process into a course.

It's called the No Phone Offer.

It'll walk you through building a sales document that does the closing for you…

One that makes your offer so clear that prospects stop asking "but will it work for me?" and start asking "how do I enroll?"

All you have to do is follow the process and you’ll get a clear, written offer that enrolls high-ticket clients for you 24/7 – even when you're not on the phone.

If you're someone who's genuinely good at what you do but gets a knot in your stomach before every discovery call or just doesn't want to waste your time on sales calls…

Click 'Learn more' to find out more about it 👉👉👉

snapshot.title: Sell High-Ticket Offers Without Sales Calls

snapshot.caption: rapidascension.freeclientsystem.com

body.text:
I don't chase high-ticket clients, haven't in over a year now and I still made $750,000 last year. What I do instead sounds a little backwards, so bear with me.

I sell a $17 product.

One, simple digital product meant to solve one very specific problem. 

It takes people about 20 minutes to go through and get a result from it.

Within about 7 days, a chunk of those $17 buyers upgrade themselves to $5,000-$10,000.

No sales calls. No "hop on a quick Zoom so I can feel you out." No convincing whatsoever.

Here's why this works (and why going straight from free content to high-ticket almost never does) 

When someone follows you for free, you're entertainment. 

You're a scroll-past. 

The second you ask them for $5,000, there's this massive trust gap you have to close. 

That's why you end up on 45-minute sales calls doing dog-and-pony shows for people who were never going to buy anyway.

But when someone pays you $17 and actually gets a quick win from it?

Something shifts.

They stop thinking of you as "some person posting content" and start thinking "this person's paid stuff actually works." 

Now there's a transaction history. 

There's trust with a receipt attached to it.

So when they see a $2,500 offer from you the following week, the internal conversation isn't "can I trust this person?" 

It's "the $17 thing worked, so..."

That's the whole game. 

The first payment does the heavy lifting. 

Everything after that has almost no friction.

I call these $17 products "Digital Snacks." 

You take one problem you've already solved in your area of expertise — something that's just sitting in your head right now — and you spend about an hour packaging it using AI prompts and plug-and-play templates.

Then you build a stupid simple landing page for it. Headline, subheadline, buy button. That's the whole thing. 

Then you run $20/day in Facebook ads. 

And within hours, people start buying. 

Then the system's built-in upgrade sequence handles the rest. Automatic. No DMs. No calls.

I tested this by going to the Sahara Desert for 5 days with zero cell service. 

Came back to 612 sales and $25K sitting in Stripe. 

Nothing needed my attention. 

Nothing broke.

Best of all, it works in the least glamorous markets as well. 

One of our students, Diego Villanueva, teaches people how to get better at chess and he does $14K/month with this. 

Starr Codd pulled in $18,600 her first week. She didn't even run ads.

1,090+ people are using the Digital Snacks system right now.

The full thing is $17 during our launch period. Normally $147 — we dropped the price temporarily while we built out more case studies. 

That price goes back up once we have enough.

If you're a coach, creator, or expert who's been giving away your best knowledge for free hoping it eventually turns into revenue... this is the part where that stops.

Click the ‘Learn more’ button to get it 👉👉👉

snapshot.linkDescription: AI-powered system. 1,090+ students. Build your first Digital Snack in 60 minutes. $17 today.
snapshot.title: How $17 Buyers Become High Ticket Clients In 7 Days (No Sales Calls)

snapshot.caption: rapidascension.freeclientsystem.com

body.text:
Every expert is optimizing for getting sales calls booked. I deleted mine completely and I made more than ever. Here's why:

I added up the hours once.

14 calls a week, about 45 minutes each when you factored in the small talk, the "tell me about your situation" part, the pitch, and the follow-up message afterward where I'd promise to "send over the details."

That's over 10 hours a week. 

That’s more than a full workday, every week, spent trying to convince semi-interested people to buy.

My close rate sat around 25%.

 So out of those 14 calls, maybe 3 or 4 would actually say yes. 

The rest needed to "think about it" or "talk to their spouse" or "check back next month."

Well, they never checked back.

About a year ago I stopped doing calls entirely, and did something better that allowed me to make well over 6-figures last year while finally having the freedom I want.

What I do instead: sell a $17 product up front. 

A Digital Snack — solves one specific problem, takes someone about 20 minutes to go through.

When a person pays $17 and gets an actual result from it, they want more.

Just like the first bite of Doritos makes you hungry for more, the first win gets them hungry for more.

As a result, you stop being "some person trying to sell me coaching" and become "the person whose stuff actually works." 

That’s why, when my high-ticket offer shows up in the automated upgrade sequence a few days later, the internal conversation is completely different and that’s why they upgrade themselves, automatically. 

I don't pitch. I don't hop on a call. 

In fact, half the time I don't even know it happened until I open Stripe.

I tested how far this could go. 

So I went to the Sahara Desert for 5 days — zero cell service, zero WiFi, zero access to anything…

…and came back to 612 new sales and $25K sitting in my account. 

The system didn't need me to be present, let alone on a phone call.

My revenue actually went up when I quit doing calls. 

That still catches me off guard when I say it out loud.

I traded 10 hours a week of convincing for a system that runs the same process automatically, around the clock.

Digital Snacks shows you exactly how to set this up, step-by-step. 

The $17 product, the landing page, the ads, the upgrade sequence — everything.

So if you're spending your weeks on Zoom with people who mostly aren't going to buy anyway... 

Let me hand you my system that will get you out of it. 

Click the ‘Learn more’ button to get started 👉👉👉

snapshot.title: How $17 Buyers Become High Ticket Clients In 7 Days (No Sales Calls)

snapshot.caption: rapidascension.freeclientsystem.com

body.text:
Diego had no audience, no email list, and no idea how to sell anything online. 6 months later he's making $14K/month teaching chess. 

Here’s how: 

He didn’t get any sponsors, he didn’t sign any brand deals, he didn’t go viral and he definitely didn’t post every day. 

Here's what he did instead: 

Diego made a $17 guide called "The Opening Trap Guide." 

It shows intermediate players how to win games in the first 10 moves using traps their opponents won't see coming.

The whole product is 20 pages long and it solves one very specific problem. 

He spends about $30/day running ads to it. 

Someone sees the ad, buys the guide, and wins their next chess game that afternoon.

But here's the part that matters.

After they buy, Diego doesn't get on a call with them, doesn't send a calendar link and doesn't try to convince them of anything.

All he does is send them a few automated emails over the next week he wrote in advance. 

Diego talks about how he thinks about chess, shares some stories and somewhere in there, he mentions his $500 coaching program for players who want to go deeper.

There’s no pitch & no pressure, just an open door…

…and about 8% of his $17 buyers walk through it.

Now here's what I want you to notice: 

Diego didn't wait, didn't spend two years "building an audience" before he let himself sell something and didn't tell himself he needed 10K followers first, or 50K, or whatever number feels safe.

He just built a $17 product, put it in front of people who wanted it, and let the system do the rest.

That’s what the ‘Rapid Ascension System' will allow you to do. 

It will walk you through creating a digital product that’s cheap enough that people buy without thinking too hard and valuable enough that customers get hungry more when they're done.

So if you've been waiting until you have "enough" followers to start making real money...

You might be standing at a door that was never locked.

It’s time to allow people to start walking through it and get the revenue & recognition you deserve.

Click ‘Learn more’ to get started 👉👉👉

body.text:
I don't chase high-ticket clients, haven't in over a year now and I still made $750,000 last year. What I do instead sounds a little backwards, so bear with me.

I sell a $17 product.

One, simple digital product meant to solve one very specific problem. 

It takes people about 20 minutes to go through and get a result from it.

Within about 7 days, a chunk of those $17 buyers upgrade themselves to $5,000-$10,000.

No sales calls. No "hop on a quick Zoom so I can feel you out." No convincing whatsoever.

Here's why this works (and why going straight from free content to high-ticket almost never does) 

When someone follows you for free, you're entertainment. 

You're a scroll-past. 

The second you ask them for $5,000, there's this massive trust gap you have to close. 

That's why you end up on 45-minute sales calls doing dog-and-pony shows for people who were never going to buy anyway.

But when someone pays you $17 and actually gets a quick win from it?

Something shifts.

They stop thinking of you as "some person posting content" and start thinking "this person's paid stuff actually works." 

Now there's a transaction history. 

There's trust with a receipt attached to it.

So when they see a $2,500 offer from you the following week, the internal conversation isn't "can I trust this person?" 

It's "the $17 thing worked, so..."

That's the whole game. 

The first payment does the heavy lifting. 

Everything after that has almost no friction.

I call these $17 products "Digital Snacks." 

You take one problem you've already solved in your area of expertise — something that's just sitting in your head right now — and you spend about an hour packaging it using AI prompts and plug-and-play templates.

Then you build a stupid simple landing page for it. Headline, subheadline, buy button. That's the whole thing. 

Then you run $20/day in Facebook ads. 

And within hours, people start buying. 

Then the system's built-in upgrade sequence handles the rest. Automatic. No DMs. No calls.

I tested this by going to the Sahara Desert for 5 days with zero cell service. 

Came back to 612 sales and $25K sitting in Stripe. 

Nothing needed my attention. 

Nothing broke.

Best of all, it works in the least glamorous markets as well. 

One of our students, Diego Villanueva, teaches people how to get better at chess and he does $14K/month with this. 

Starr Codd pulled in $18,600 her first week. She didn't even run ads.

1,090+ people are using the Digital Snacks system right now.

The full thing is $17 during our launch period. Normally $147 — we dropped the price temporarily while we built out more case studies. 

That price goes back up once we have enough.

If you're a coach, creator, or expert who's been giving away your best knowledge for free hoping it eventually turns into revenue... this is the part where that stops.

Click the ‘Learn more’ button to get it 👉👉👉

body.text:
If you're an expert already making $10K, $20K, maybe $30K/month...what got you here won't get you to $100K/month.

And you probably already know that deep down.

You've felt it for a while now, haven't you?

That nagging feeling that something's off...

That you're working harder than you should be for the money you're making…

That there's a ceiling above you and no matter how much you push, it won't budge.

Here's what's going on (and what you can do about it):

The hustle that built your business is the same thing KEEPING you stuck.

I know that sounds backwards, but stay with me for a second.

You see, you got here by being good at what you do. 

By showing up when others didn't, by personally selling and by getting on calls and closing deals yourself.

That worked. 

You should be proud of it, but…

A different problem walked in the door:

You wake up and your calendar is already full. 

Calls stacked on calls stacked on calls. 

The revenue doesn't show up unless you show up. 

And while one month you made good money…

The next month you have to make it all over again, from scratch, from the same audience that's getting a liiiittle more tired of being sold to.

That's a job you created that happens to own you. 

And here's what makes this particularly frustrating...

You've watched other experts - some of them honestly less talented than you - figure something out that lets them scale past you. 

They're not working more hours, but somehow they broke through the ceiling you're still hitting.

So what did they figure out?

They stopped being the engine and they built a second one.

See, most experts try to scale by doing MORE of what's already working. More content. More launches. More calls. More hours. They're playing harder within the same game.

But the experts who actually break $100K/month? 

They changed the rules.

Instead of only selling to their existing audience, they built an automated acquisition system that brings in new buyers every single day—without launches, without calls, without personal energy.

It starts with a $17 product that solves one specific problem. 

Something small enough that complete strangers buy it without needing to know who you are. 

Then they run cheap ads to it, allowing them to acquire 50, 100, 200 new buyers every single day. 

That’s how they build a list of buyers who already paid them, trust them.

Those buyers get a short automated email sequence over the next week. 

Nothing aggressive. 

Just more value, more insight, and eventually an invitation to go deeper with a higher-ticket offer.

The $17 product acquires customers while you sleep…

The emails upgrade them while you sleep…

And you wake up to Stripe notifications you didn't personally create.

That's the second engine.

One of our members - a business consultant - was stuck at $27K/month for almost a year. 

He couldn't figure out how to grow without taking more calls. 

60 days after installing this system, he crossed $80K.

Not because he worked harder, because he finally had something working when he wasn't.

That's what I'm going to show you inside Digital Snacks.

I’m going to give you everything you need to install the growth engine under your business that allows our students to scale (see the next page). 

Click the ‘Learn more’ button to get it 👉👉👉

snapshot.linkDescription: AI-powered system. 1,090+ students. Build your first Digital Snack in 60 minutes. $17 today.
snapshot.title: How $17 Buyers Become High Ticket Clients In 7 Days (No Sales Calls)

snapshot.caption: phonefreesales.co

body.text:
I used to customize my offer for every single High-Ticket prospect.

"Let me understand your situation first, then I'll tailor a solution for you."

I thought that's what high-ticket required. Personalization. Customization. Bespoke solutions.

But it was killing me.

Every prospect needed a different pitch. A different proposal. A different price point.

I was rebuilding my offer from scratch on every single call.

Then it hit me:

The reason I needed to customize wasn't because my clients needed different things.

It's because my offer was too broad.

Think about it:

If your offer needs to be customized for each client, it's not an offer—it's a consulting engagement.

If you need to "tailor" it based on their situation, you don't have a product.

If every client gets something different, you can't scale.

The problem wasn't my clients. The problem was my offer.

So I did something radical.

I created ONE offer. One clear, specific, non-negotiable offer.

Same outcome. Same process. Same price. For everyone.

No customization. No "let's tailor this for you." No "it depends."

Just: here's what you get, here's how it works, here's what it costs.

I put it in a document and sent it to my next 10 leads.

I didn't ask about their situation. I didn't customize anything. I just sent the same doc to everyone.

And something amazing happened:

5 of them bought. $75,000 in revenue. Zero customization.

They didn't need a bespoke solution. They needed a clear one.

That's when I realized:

Clarity scales. Customization doesn't.

And once I figured this out, everything changed:

→ I stopped spending hours "tailoring" proposals
→ My close rate went UP (because the offer was clearer)
→ My delivery became easier (same process for everyone)
→ I could finally scale without burning out

This is what No-Phone Offers do.

They're ONE clear offer that works for everyone. No customization. No tailoring. Just clarity.

The No-Phone Offer Method shows you exactly how to do this:

✅ How to create ONE offer that works for your entire market
✅ How to eliminate the need for customization
✅ How to scale your offers without scaling your time
✅ How to close high-ticket clients with the same doc every time

This is the same method I used to go from customizing every offer to sending the same doc to everyone—and 5X my close rate.

Right now, you can get the complete training for $9 (normally $97).

Click below. Get instant access. Stop customizing. Start scaling.

→ Get The No-Phone Offer Method for $9

30-Day Money-Back Guarantee. 1,090+ verified students. 4.7/5 rating.

P.S. The best part? I send the exact same document to cold leads and warm referrals. Same doc. That's the power of a clear, non-customized offer.

One of our students, Diego Villanueva, teaches people how to get better at chess and he does $14K/month with this. 

Starr Codd pulled in $18,600 her first week. She didn't even run ads.

1,090+ people are using the Digital Snacks system right now.

The full thing is $17 during our launch period. Normally $147 — we dropped the price temporarily while we built out more case studies. 

That price goes back up once we have enough.

If you're a coach, creator, or expert who's been giving away your best knowledge for free hoping it eventually turns into revenue... this is the part where that stops.

Click the ‘Learn more’ button to get it 👉👉👉

snapshot.title: How $17 Buyers Become High Ticket Clients In 7 Days (No Sales Calls)

snapshot.caption: phonefreesales.co

body.text:
I removed sales calls from my business 6 months ago – my close rate more than doubled.

I'm a great coach. 

I know that because my clients get results. 

They refer friends, they stay for months, they re-enroll.

But put me on a sales call… and something breaks.

My voice gets tight, I talk too fast, I start over-explaining things nobody asked about and I can feel the prospect pulling away, so I over-explain MORE — which only makes it worse.

For 3 years I thought the answer was getting better at sales calls. 

So I bought a closing course. 

Then another one. 

Practiced objection handling in front of my bathroom mirror like some kind of lunatic.

And while I got slightly better… I disliked every second of it.

My prospects could smell it, too. 

My close rate sat around 15%, which is pretty rough when your whole income depends on it.

One night after a particularly bad call — the kind where you hang up and just sit there replaying everything you said wrong — I started thinking about why my coaching sessions felt so natural but sales calls felt like torture.

And I realized something obvious that I'd been ignoring for years.

In my coaching sessions, I prepare.

I think through what I'm going to say, I write notes and I organize my thoughts. 

Everything comes out clear and confident because I've already worked through it in writing. 

On sales calls? 

I wing it and hope my personality carries me. It doesn't.

So I asked myself a question that probably should've been obvious a lot sooner: 

What if I just stopped selling on the phone and sold the way I actually communicate well?

And that’s exactly what I did.

I created one clear sales document that laid out four things: 

👉 The exact problem I solve
👉 The exact mechanism I use
👉 The exact outcome clients get
👉 And the exact process we'd follow together.

That's it. 

No pitch and no performance. 

Just the information someone actually needs to make a decision.

If they have questions, they send me a message and I answer them. 

If it's right, they enroll. 

If not, neither of us wasted an hour pretending to enjoy a Zoom call.

Within 3 months, my close rate went from 15% to 38%.

The only thing that changed was the format.

And something else changed that I didn't expect — my authority went UP. 

Turns out, when you stop chasing people on calls and let a document speak for you…

Prospects treat you differently – and you feel like an expert again (instead of a salesperson auditioning for a job).

A handful of other coaches saw what I was doing and asked me to walk them through it. 

Enough of them asked that I eventually put the whole process into a course.

It's called the No Phone Offer.

It'll walk you through building a sales document that does the closing for you…

One that makes your offer so clear that prospects stop asking "but will it work for me?" and start asking "how do I enroll?"

All you have to do is follow the process and you’ll get a clear, written offer that enrolls high-ticket clients for you 24/7 – even when you're not on the phone.

If you're someone who's genuinely good at what you do but gets a knot in your stomach before every discovery call or just doesn't want to waste your time on sales calls…

Click 'Learn more' to find out more about it 👉👉👉
